text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Gerald T. Holloway
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Male
ORDERING PROVIDER: [Interventional Radiology / Medical Oncology]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist on Service]
CLINICAL TRIAL REFERENCE: [KRAS G12D Inhibitor Phase I/II Protocol -- Correlative Biopsy]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN INFORMATION

Specimen ID: [SP-XXXXX]
Accession Number: [REDACTED]
Date of Procedure: [See chart]
Date Received in Pathology: [See chart]
Date of Final Report: [See chart]
Procedure Type: CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy
Anatomic Site: Right lower lobe pulmonary nodule
Laterality: Right
Clinical Indication: Known metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma (KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS), progressive disease on second-line FOLFIRI/ramucirumab. Re-biopsy performed for clinical trial eligibility confirmation and correlative molecular studies (KRAS G12D inhibitor Phase I/II trial). Pulmonary nodule right lower lobe measuring 0.9 cm on most recent CT, new/growing compared to prior imaging.
Submitting Service: Interventional Radiology
Submitted Material: 3 core needle biopsy fragments in formalin, labeled "right lower lobe lung nodule."
Clinical History: 59-year-old male with metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma, sigmoid primary, KRAS G12D-mutant, microsatellite stable. Prior resection of primary tumor. Previously treated with FOLFOX6/bevacizumab (partial response, discontinued oxaliplatin due to grade 2-3 cumulative peripheral neuropathy), maintenance 5-FU/bevacizumab (disease progression with new pulmonary nodules), and FOLFIRI/ramucirumab (partial response followed by progressive disease). Now being evaluated for enrollment onto investigational KRAS G12D-selective inhibitor trial. Re-biopsy required per protocol to confirm KRAS G12D mutation in metastatic setting and exclude newly acquired resistance alterations.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION

Received in formalin, labeled "right lower lobe lung nodule -- core biopsy," are three tan-gray cylindrical tissue cores. Core 1 measures 1.1 cm in length x 0.1 cm in diameter. Core 2 measures 0.9 cm in length x 0.1 cm in diameter. Core 3 measures 0.8 cm in length x 0.1 cm in diameter. The cores have a firm, slightly firm consistency. No gross necrosis or hemorrhage is discernible at this scale. All three cores are submitted entirely: cores 1 and 2 submitted in cassette A (sections for H&E and potential IHC), core 3 submitted in cassette B (FFPE tissue reserved for molecular studies per clinical trial protocol). Three H&E levels prepared from cassette A.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION

Sections from cassette A (cores 1 and 2) show fragments of lung parenchyma with near-complete replacement by a moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma. The neoplastic cells are arranged in irregular glandular and cribriform structures, with focal solid nesting and central comedonecrosis ("dirty necrosis" pattern). Individual tumor cells are columnar to cuboidal with moderately enlarged, hyperchromatic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and a moderate amount of amphophilic cytoplasm. Intraluminal necrotic debris is present within glandular lumina, a morphologic pattern highly characteristic of colorectal-type adenocarcinoma. Mitotic figures are readily identified (estimated 6-8 per 10 high-power fields). Background viable lung parenchyma is present at the core periphery, showing mild reactive type II pneumocyte hyperplasia and focal hemosiderin deposition consistent with prior procedural change; no primary lung carcinoma morphology (no lepidic, acinar-predominant primary pattern, no squamous differentiation, no neuroendocrine features) is identified.

There is no evidence of visceral pleural involvement in the sampled material. Lymphovascular invasion is identified focally within the biopsy fragments. Perineural invasion is not identified in the sampled material.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (ANCILLARY STUDIES)

A directed immunohistochemical panel was performed to characterize the tumor and confirm colorectal primary origin:

  CK20 (clone Ks20.8): POSITIVE -- diffuse strong cytoplasmic/membranous staining in tumor cells.
  CDX2 (clone EPR2764Y): POSITIVE -- strong diffuse nuclear staining in tumor cells.
  CK7 (clone OV-TL 12/30): FOCALLY POSITIVE -- weak patchy staining in a minority of tumor cells.
  TTF-1 (clone 8G7G3/1): NEGATIVE -- no nuclear staining in tumor cells (positive internal control in background pneumocytes).
  Napsin A: NEGATIVE -- no cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells.
  p40: NEGATIVE -- no nuclear staining in tumor cells.
  Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE.
  Chromogranin A: NEGATIVE.
  SATB2 (clone EP281): POSITIVE -- nuclear staining in tumor cells (supports lower gastrointestinal origin).

IMPRESSION (IHC): The immunophenotype (CK20+, CDX2+, SATB2+, CK7 focally+, TTF-1 negative, Napsin A negative, p40 negative) is consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma of colorectal origin. A primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma is not supported by the morphologic and immunohistochemical findings.

MISMATCH REPAIR (MMR) PROTEIN EXPRESSION BY IHC:

MMR IHC performed per clinical trial protocol for MSI status confirmation:
  MLH1 (clone ES05): RETAINED nuclear expression in tumor cells.
  MSH2 (clone FE11): RETAINED nuclear expression in tumor cells.
  MSH6 (clone EP49): RETAINED nuclear expression in tumor cells.
  PMS2 (clone EP51): RETAINED nuclear expression in tumor cells.

INTERPRETATION: Intact (retained) mismatch repair protein expression in all four proteins. This pattern is consistent with microsatellite-stable (MSS) tumor, concordant with prior testing on the primary sigmoid colon resection specimen and the initial colonoscopic biopsy. MSI-H/dMMR tumor is NOT supported.

MOLECULAR PATHOLOGY -- KRAS MUTATION ANALYSIS (HOTSPOT/EXTENDED RAS PANEL, FFPE):

DNA extracted from FFPE tissue (cassette B, core 3). Tumor cellularity on H&E review of representative section: approximately 65-70% neoplastic nuclei; adequate for molecular analysis.

  KRAS exon 2 (codons 12/13): MUTATION DETECTED -- p.G12D (c.35G>A). Variant allele frequency: 38%. This is the identical KRAS G12D hotspot mutation identified in the primary sigmoid colon tumor biopsy (VAF 41%) and confirmed by comprehensive NGS panel at diagnosis. The slight reduction in VAF is within expected range given intratumoral heterogeneity and biopsy sampling variation.
  KRAS exon 3 (codon 61): Wild-type.
  KRAS exon 4 (codons 117, 146): Wild-type.
  NRAS exon 2 (codons 12/13): Wild-type. No acquired NRAS mutation detected.
  NRAS exon 3 (codon 61): Wild-type.
  NRAS exon 4 (codons 117, 146): Wild-type.
  BRAF V600E (codon 600): NOT DETECTED. Wild-type. (Note: BRAF V600E would represent an acquired resistance mechanism and would affect clinical trial eligibility.)
  KRAS amplification by IHC/FISH: Not performed on this specimen; prior NGS did not demonstrate KRAS amplification. Per protocol, reflex KRAS copy number analysis may be requested if clinically indicated.

ADDITIONAL MOLECULAR STUDIES: Extended panel for acquired anti-EGFR resistance alterations (KRAS G12C conversion, ERBB2 amplification, ERBB3 mutation) is PENDING at time of this report. Results to be appended as addendum upon completion.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS

RIGHT LOWER LOBE LUNG, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

METASTATIC MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED ADENOCARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH COLORECTAL PRIMARY ORIGIN.

  -- Morphology: Conventional glandular/cribriform architecture with dirty (intraluminal) necrosis, characteristic of colorectal carcinoma.
  -- Immunoprofile: CK20+, CDX2+, SATB2+, CK7 focally+, TTF-1 negative, Napsin A negative; consistent with colorectal primary, primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma excluded.
  -- Mismatch repair protein status: INTACT (MLH1+, MSH2+, MSH6+, PMS2+) -- microsatellite stable (MSS).
  -- KRAS mutation status: KRAS p.G12D (c.35G>A) DETECTED, VAF 38%.
  -- No acquired RAS/RAF resistance mutations identified (NRAS wild-type, BRAF V600E not detected).
  -- KRAS G12C: NOT detected (relevant as some KRAS G12D-directed agents may have differential off-target activity).
  -- Additional acquired resistance mutation panel: PENDING (see above).

CLINICOPATHOLOGIC COMMENT:
This CT-guided biopsy of a right lower lobe pulmonary nodule confirms metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma with preserved KRAS G12D mutation and microsatellite stable status, concordant with prior molecular characterization of the primary tumor. The absence of NRAS, BRAF V600E, or other detected secondary resistance alterations at this time supports eligibility for enrollment onto the investigational KRAS G12D-selective inhibitor clinical trial, pending completion of extended resistance panel studies (addendum to follow). These findings are consistent with KRAS G12D-driven metastatic progression without evidence of alternate resistance pathway engagement in the sampled material.

Note: Molecular findings should be interpreted in conjunction with the full clinical and radiologic context. This report does not constitute a clinical management recommendation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNED:
[Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Institution Name]

ELECTRONICALLY VERIFIED AND FINALIZED

Addendum pending: Extended resistance mutation panel (KRAS amplification, ERBB2 amplification, ERBB3 mutation, KRAS G12C secondary conversion) -- results to be reported separately.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
